name: Traditional Scottish Shortbread
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the butter until smooth.
2. Gradually add powdered sugar and continue beating to a creamy consistency.
3. Mix in flour until well combined, being careful not to overwork the dough or let the butter become oily.
4. Shape the dough into a disk, wrap in plastic, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
5. Preheat oven to 300°F (150°C).
6. Roll out the chilled dough on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of either:
- 1/4 to 1/2 inch, then cut into small rounds (approximately 1 1/2 inches) and prick twice with a fork.
- 3/4 inch thick, pinch the edges, and prick all over with a fork.
7. Bake for 20-25 minutes at 300°F (150°C), or until lightly golden brown.
8. For thicker shortbread, bake an additional 45-60 minutes at 300°F (150°C) after reducing the temperature to 375°F (190°C).
Note: Do not overwork the dough as it can become oily and affect texture.
